Improvements: widen sidewalks (10→14 ft), underground utilities, LED street lights, street trees (40 trees), rain gardens, public art, parklets (3 total), wayfinding, accessible ramps. Parking: redesign surface lot into public plaza. Funding: $8M (city $2M TIF, state/federal grants $4M, public-private $2M). Year 1: Design, Year 2–3: construction.

initiatives.title: Transportation Network Expansion: Regional Connector Roads
initiatives.description: Build 2–3 new roads connecting industrial parks + downtown to US-77/main regional corridors, reducing traffic congestion.
initiatives.whyMatters: Road connectivity = site competitiveness. 10-minute faster commute = $500K–$1M cost savings for relocating firm.
initiatives.implementation: Develop 'El Dorado Transportation Network Plan' ($75K). Identify: bottleneck intersections, industrial park connectivity gaps, downtown bypass alternative. Priority projects: bypass connector (3 miles, $4M), industrial park loop (2 miles, $3M). Funding: city bonds, state transportation grants. Year 1: Plan adopted, grant applications. Year 2–4: construction phased.

initiatives.title: Parks & Recreation Master Plan: 10-Year Investment Strategy
initiatives.description: Comprehensive parks plan identifying needs (120 residents per acre park rule suggests 3–5 new parks) + 10-year development roadmap.
initiatives.whyMatters: Parks = recruitment tool + health driver + property value enhancer (+5–10%). Plan enables coordinated investment vs. ad-hoc.
initiatives.implementation: Commission 'El Dorado Parks & Recreation Master Plan' ($100K). Analysis: current acreage, accessibility gaps, demographic needs, sports/rec demand. Recommendations: 3–4 new parks (2–3 acres each), playground upgrades (5 parks), trail expansion (10 miles), aquatics facility feasibility. Investment: $5M–$8M over 10 years. Year 1: Plan adopted, Phase 1 projects funded.

initiatives.title: Public School Facilities Assessment & Modernization Strategy
initiatives.description: Comprehensive audit of USD 490 facilities (3 schools) + 10-year capital improvement plan addressing aging infrastructure.
initiatives.whyMatters: School quality = biggest driver of residential attraction. Modernized facilities improve test scores 5–10% + attract 50+ families/year.
initiatives.implementation: Commission 'USD 490 Facilities Master Plan' ($50K, joint city-school funding). Scope: all 3 buildings, condition assessment, code compliance, learning environment upgrades, technology infrastructure, accessibility. Capital needs: $20M–$30M (estimate). Funding strategy: bond, grants, public-private partnerships. City role: support bonding, coordinate with economic development. Year 1: Plan complete, bond strategy developed.

initiatives.title: Economic Development Impact Fee Structure
initiatives.description: Establish tiered impact fees on new commercial/industrial development funding infrastructure serving growth.
initiatives.whyMatters: Impact fees ensure growth pays for growth infrastructure. $20K–$50K per development funds roadways, water, sewer, parks needed for expansion.
initiatives.implementation: Adopt 'El Dorado Development Impact Fee Ordinance' (professional study $30K). Structure: commercial development ($2–$5/sq ft), industrial ($1–$3/sq ft), multifamily ($1.5K–$3K per unit). Uses: infrastructure tied to growth (roads, water, sewer, parks). Revenue potential: $200K–$500K/year. Exemptions: qualified economic development projects (TIF, big recruitment).

initiatives.title: Facade Grant Program for Historic Preservation
initiatives.description: Annual $300K–$500K program offering $10K–$30K grants for exterior restoration of historic buildings (downtown priority).
initiatives.whyMatters: Historic buildings = character draw. Facade grants 5:1 ROI (grant funds trigger $5–$10 private investment). Programs operate successfully 200+ communities.
initiatives.implementation: Establish 'El Dorado Historic Preservation Facade Grant Program'. Grant: $10K–$30K per project (50% match). Eligible: buildings 50+ years old, character-defining features. Uses: siding, windows, doors, roofs, storefronts, signage. Application: competitive. Budget: $400K annual (grants), $75K admin. Year 1: 15–20 projects, $5M private investment triggered.

initiatives.title: Community Benefit Agreement (CBA) Framework
initiatives.description: Establish CBA process for major developments (mega sites, large employers) ensuring community benefits (jobs, housing, infrastructure).
initiatives.whyMatters: CBAs formalize private-public partnership expectations. Projects with CBAs achieve 20–30% higher community satisfaction + political support.
initiatives.implementation: Adopt 'El Dorado CBA Policy' (model framework $30K). Process: community engagement before development approval, negotiated benefits tied to development size/impact. Typical benefits: 30% local hiring pledge, affordable housing (10–20% units), public space, job training fund, infrastructure improvements. Execution: binding legal agreement. Apply to mega site projects (100+ jobs, $10M+ investment).

initiatives.title: Regional Logistics & Transportation Coordination
initiatives.description: Work with 3–4 county governments to coordinate logistics infrastructure (industrial parks, rail, highway access) across region.
initiatives.whyMatters: Regional coordination enables mega projects too big for single county. Logistics corridor attracting $100M+ investment across region.
initiatives.implementation: Establish 'Butler County Regional Logistics Council' (elected officials + ED directors). Coordination: infrastructure master planning (rail access, highway improvements, park development), joint marketing, cross-county incentive consistency. Focus: US-77 corridor as regional advantage (vs. I-35 congestion). Year 1: council formed, regional plan adopted, 2 prospect recruits.

initiatives.title: Regional Workforce Training Hub & Career Pathways
initiatives.description: Establish regional training facility offering industry-aligned programs (healthcare, manufacturing, trades) with guaranteed job placement.
initiatives.whyMatters: Coordinated training eliminates duplication, pools resources. One regional hub serves 50K+ region vs. fragmented programs.
initiatives.implementation: Develop 'Butler County Regional Training Hub' (partnership with Butler CC, USD 490, employers). Facility: shared training campus (100K sq ft). Programs: healthcare (CNA→RN pathway), manufacturing (CNC→engineering pathway), trades (apprenticeships). Enrollment: 500+/year. Job placement: 85%+ rate. Funding: state workforce training grants. Year 1: facility planning, grant applications.

initiatives.title: Regional Healthcare System Strengthening
initiatives.description: Coordinate with county health system on integrated urgent care + telemedicine + rural clinic network across region.
initiatives.whyMatters: Rural health crisis = opportunity. Integrated system improves access, reduces costs, attracts providers.
initiatives.implementation: Establish 'Butler County Healthcare Coalition' (hospitals, clinics, ED directors). Strategy: regional telehealth hub (reduces isolation), urgent care network (El Dorado + 2 towns), specialist recruitment (cardiologists, surgeons), rural clinic support. Investment: $10M–$15M (phased). Year 1: strategy developed, grant applications submitted.
